channel.title: NPR Topics: News
channel.description: NPR news, audio, and podcasts. Coverage of breaking stories, national and world news, politics, business, science, technology, and extended coverage of major national and world events.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: Knicks beat Spurs by one point for 2-0 lead in NBA Finals
item.description: The red-hot Knicks are going home, two wins away from an NBA championship that the capital of the world has been waiting to see for generations.
item.content:encoded:
The red-hot Knicks are going home, two wins away from an NBA championship that the capital of the world has been waiting to see for generations.

(Image credit: Eric Gay)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: U.S. military says it shot down Iranian drones launched toward Gulf allies
item.description: The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict.
item.content:encoded:
The exchange of strikes comes as the Trump administration ramps up pressure on Iran to make a deal to end the conflict.

(Image credit: Mark Schiefelbein)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Democrat Xavier Becerra wins the top spot in November's race for California governor
item.description: Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has clinched the top spot on California's ballot for governor. With millions of ballots still to be counted, his November challenger is unknown.
item.content:encoded:
Former Health and Human Services Secretary Xavier Becerra has clinched the top spot on California's ballot for governor. With millions of ballots still to be counted, his November challenger is unknown.

(Image credit: Mario Tama)
item.dc:creator: Marisa Lagos

item.title: After D.C.'s Reflecting Pool gets repainted, visitors ask: What changed?
item.description: The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.
item.content:encoded:
The Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool is refilling after President Trump had it painted "American flag blue." Some visitors say the results of the project — which reportedly cost millions — are subtle.

(Image credit: Rahmat Gul)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: CDC report: Ebola outbreak could rival the worst on record unless world acts
item.description: New modeling from the CDC shows that if measures aren't taken immediately, this outbreak could sicken more than 20,000 people in the next three months.
item.content:encoded:
New modeling from the CDC shows that if measures aren't taken immediately, this outbreak could sicken more than 20,000 people in the next three months.

(Image credit: Daniel Buuma)
item.dc:creator: Rob Stein

item.title: Graham Platner isn't going anywhere in Maine Senate race after latest controversy
item.description: Graham Platner is denying accusations of being physically rough with former girlfriends saying that report in The New York Times and other controversies are a sign his campaign is gaining momentum.
item.content:encoded:
Graham Platner is denying accusations of being physically rough with former girlfriends saying that report in The New York Times and other controversies are a sign his campaign is gaining momentum.

(Image credit: Joe Raedle)
item.dc:creator: Saige Miller

item.title: Xi Jinping will travel to North Korea next week in first visit since 2019
item.description: The announcement was made by both countries Friday a day after North Korea unveiled a new facility to produce nuclear fuel.
item.content:encoded:
The announcement was made by both countries Friday a day after North Korea unveiled a new facility to produce nuclear fuel.

(Image credit: Ahn Young-joon)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: South Africa rolls out game-changing HIV shot amid funding shortfalls
item.description: A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.
item.content:encoded:
A new twice-yearly HIV prevention injection could transform South Africa's fight against the epidemic — but U.S. aid cuts and limited doses threaten to slow its impact.

(Image credit: Ihsaan Haffe)
item.dc:creator: Kate Bartlett

item.title: The U.S. adds 172,000 jobs. Many are in restaurants, bars and hotels
item.description: U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.
item.content:encoded:
U.S. employers added jobs for the third month in a row in May, while the unemployment rate held steady at 4.3%. But wage gains softened and likely failed to keep pace with rising prices.

(Image credit: Brandon Bell)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley

item.title: China's highest bridge brings tourists and internet access to remote communities
item.description: A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.
item.content:encoded:
A link to the highway that cuts travel times from hours to just minutes, and a symbol of a flow of investment that has provided unprecedented access to high-speed internet in this remote region.

(Image credit: Ng Han Guan)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press
